FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Fintech and tech giants pursue banking charters while regional M&A continues; Fed officials remain split on rate trajectory.

bullets.text: Klarna applies for ILC charter to offer deposits and lending directly under FDIC regulation, escalating fintech's push into traditional banking.
Sony lands conditional OCC approval for stablecoin-focused trust charter (Connectia Trust), launching in 2027.
Fed officials deeply divided on rate cuts per FOMC minutes—hawks vs. doves tension likely to persist across multiple meetings.
Regional M&A active: Hometown Bank (MA) acquiring NH peer for $160M; Bank7 pursues 71% Century Bank stake via forced collateral sale.

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: RIA consolidation accelerates while aggregation platforms stumble; Fisher Investments defies trend with organic mega-growth.

bullets.text: Morningstar's ByAllAccounts sale to Pello collapses—another aggregation sector casualty as Plaid eyes IPO and CFPB mulls allowing data fees, signaling structural stress in the category.
RIA M&A in overdrive: Wavvest poaches Schwab's $1.4B advisor; Choreo snaps up $700M planning-focused shop; Goldman's custody gaining share from brand/UHNW positioning.
Fisher Investments hit ~$450B AUM (up from $112B in 2019) through pure organic growth and media blitz—rejecting consolidation while rivals pursue M&A.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: Reinsurance capacity pressures intensify as US MGA market booms, while Middle East shipping risks spike and specialty consolidation accelerates.

bullets.text: Gallagher Re's ceded premiums up 18% YoY, driven by US MGA expansion signaling sustained specialty capacity demand.
RGA pushing into Japan life reinsurance via coinsurance blocks with Sumitomo Life and Tokio Marine, broadening regional footprint.
Qatari LNG tanker hit off Oman; Iran targeting additional vessels in Hormuz—shipping disruption and marine claims exposure mounting.
Specialty MGA consolidation continues: Nirvana acquiring Ryan Specialty's Swedish unit; Acrisure launching UK HNW practice post-Smith Greenfield deal.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: JPMorgan and BofA pursue Fiserv debit network; Equifax enters Mexico; capital rotates into AI data centers.

bullets.text: JPMorgan and BofA in early-stage talks to acquire Fiserv's debit payments network; no valuation or timeline yet disclosed.
Equifax spending $750M on Círculo de Crédito to establish Mexican footprint in high-growth credit bureau market.
CMBS capital significantly rotating from traditional CRE into spec data center financing as AI infrastructure demand accelerates.
ECB mandating banks submit AI cyber-threat mitigation plans, flagging emerging risks to IT resilience from advanced models.

PE & Deals.lede: LPs rebalancing portfolios toward alternatives while GPs adapt fund structures; secondaries market gaining traction despite DPI headwinds.

bullets.text: Rest (Australia's 4th-largest pension) expanding PE and infrastructure allocations under new leadership, signaling institutional appetite for alternatives in APAC.
Temasek admitting EMEA direct investment underperformance; pivoting toward AI, private credit, and core-plus infrastructure—a major LP recalibration for European GPs.
Patria closes $677m secondaries fund targeting smaller deals as DPI challenges persist; retail capital emerging as alternative source despite regulatory friction.
ILPA updates carried interest guidance on 'remain in place' language, reflecting structural shifts in secondaries and continuation vehicle economics.

Markets & Macro.lede: UniCredit eyes Commerzbank control amid German political friction, while UK capital markets show stark M&A-over-IPO preference and credit fundamentals decoupling from technicals.

bullets.text: UniCredit clinches 48% of Commerzbank after successful tender, now positioned for majority control of Germany's second-largest bank—triggering Berlin pushback.
UK M&A bid value hits £60bn vs. £2.2bn in new IPO listings (27:1 ratio), signaling structural capital flight from primary equity markets to consolidation.
Credit markets decoupling from fundamentals as passive flows and algos drive pricing on momentum rather than quality—a 'Newton's Cradle' dynamic masking underlying risk.
UK pension/insurance funds rotating gilts into AI-linked bonds as BoE holds rates, creating narrative-driven asset class disconnected from traditional credit metrics.

Newsletters.lede: Banks scheme to sidestep fee caps, wealth managers chase new tax angles, SEC loosens accreditation rules, and single-stock ETFs proliferate.

bullets.text: Wall Street banks exploring card payment network acquisitions to escape interchange fee caps that have drained tens of billions from issuers—a structural workaround to regulatory pressure.
Direct-indexing's tax-loss harvesting edge fading as markets rise; wealth managers pivoting to long-short SMAs to preserve the tax-alpha narrative that justifies fee collection.
SEC proposal would grant accredited investor status based on advisor relationship alone, bypassing net-worth/income thresholds—advisor community split on access expansion vs. compliance risk.
Single-stock ETF filings accelerating around SK Hynix IPO and other debuts, following SpaceX's IPO-driven filing surge; State Street's SPYM picked as core Trump admin government account holding.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: Four thought pieces on institutional innovation—prediction markets proving their mettle, AI governance raising legitimacy questions, and a forgotten engineering ambition that shaped cities.

bullets.text: Maine Democratic primary prediction market swung from 9% to 96% dropout probability in one day on scandal news, showing real-time info aggregation that could reshape institutional decision-making beyond politics.

8% of major US coastal city land was reclaimed from water in 1890s–1920s; we stopped post-WWII despite housing constraints, signaling a collapse in engineering ambition or regulatory tolerance worth understanding.

AI governance frameworks like Claude's Constitution raise real questions about consent and legitimacy—a constitutional theory problem financial institutions should grapple with as AI becomes operationally critical.

all.lede: M&A fever across fintech and payments infrastructure—Klarna chases banking charter, JPM/BofA eye Fiserv debit network, UniCredit grabs 48% of Commerzbank—while credit markets show troubling disconnect from fundamentals.

bullets.text: Klarna ILC play: Swedish fintech applies for FDIC charter to take deposits and lend directly in the U.S., sidestepping traditional banking partnerships.
JPMorgan and BofA in talks to acquire Fiserv's debit network—part of broader Wall Street push to own payments infrastructure and escape fee caps costing billions annually.
UniCredit now holds 48% of Commerzbank after tender offer succeeds; German political backlash mounting as path to majority control emerges.
Credit markets show Newton's Cradle problem: passive flows and algorithmic trading decoupled from fundamentals, with momentum—not credit quality—driving price discovery.
